question.text: Under SEBI's accredited investor framework for AIFs, an individual must have annual income of at least Rs. 2 crore, OR net worth of at least:

answer.text: Rs. 5 crore with at least Rs. 2.5 crore in financial assets
Rs. 7.5 crore with at least Rs. 3.75 crore in financial assets
Rs. 10 crore with at least Rs. 5 crore in financial assets
Rs. 25 crore regardless of asset composition

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Accredited investor criteria: Annual Income ≥ Rs. 2 crore, OR Net Worth ≥ Rs. 7.5 crore (with ≥ Rs. 3.75 crore in financial assets), OR combination of Income ≥ Rs. 1 crore + Net Worth ≥ Rs. 5 crore (≥ Rs. 2.5 crore in financial assets). Accredited investors can invest in some AIFs with a reduced minimum of Rs. 25 lakh. **The Trap:** The financial asset sub-requirement (Rs. 3.75 crore) is key — you can't qualify with Rs. 7.5 crore in just real estate. **Cross-Exam Context:** Accredited investor criteria are tested in X-A for understanding AIF access provisions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Accredited investor status allows lower ticket size access (Rs. 25 lakh vs Rs. 1 crore) — but the reduced minimum doesn't reduce the risk. Ensure clients genuinely meet criteria before pursuing this route.
